findings.evidence: `TauCeti.twoRank` is declared with `[Finite G]` at line 162, while Mathlib's needed theorem is `Module.natCard_eq_pow_finrank [Module.Finite K V]` in `Mathlib/FieldTheory/Finiteness.lean:97`, so finiteness of all of `G` is stronger than the theorem requires.
findings.fix: State `twoRank` under `[Module.Finite (ZMod 2) (ElementaryTwoQuotient G)]` (or `FiniteDimensional`) and make the cardinality theorem use the same assumption, leaving `[Finite G]` only as an instance-producing special case.
findings.issue: `twoRank` and `card_elementaryTwoQuotient_eq_two_pow_twoRank` require `[Finite G]`, although the natural assumption is only finite-dimensionality/finite generation of the `ZMod 2` quotient; this blocks legitimate infinite groups with finite `G/G²`.

summary: The quotient construction is at the right general group level, but the exported rank API is unnecessarily restricted to finite groups rather than finite-dimensional quotients.
